content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be augmented by designing memory as a distributed A2A protocol, where individual memory chunks act as autonomous active learning agents that negotiate with each other to decide which unlabeled experiences get encoded into long-term storage rather than having this selection controlled by a central agent. This distributed memory architecture leverages agent-to-agent negotiation to dynamically prioritize encoding based on cross-memory context that a central active learning selector would miss.
